message:
feat(tui): add vim-idiomatic keybindings for tree navigation and search (#8)

Addresses the full scope of beads_viewer_rust#8 with independent implementation:

Tree search:
- `/` in Tree view activates search-as-you-type filtering over tree nodes
- `n`/`N` cycle through matches, `Enter` confirms, `Esc` cancels
- Search hit indicators shown inline on matching tree rows
- Search banner with match count displayed at top of tree panel

Vim fold commands (z-prefix):
- `zo` expand fold under cursor, `zc` close fold under cursor
- `za` toggle fold (same as Enter, but standard vim binding)
- `zR` expand all folds, `zM` collapse all folds
- `zz` recenter viewport on cursor

gg jump-to-top:
- Rapid `gg` (double-tap) jumps to top of current list in any view mode
- Uses latch-based detection: first `g` toggles graph speculatively,
  second `g` within the same key sequence undoes the toggle and jumps
- Single `g` followed by any other key preserves existing graph toggle

Full page scroll:
- `Ctrl-F` / `Ctrl-B` wired as full-page down/up across all view modes
  (List, Detail, Board, Tree) — completes the vim scroll set alongside
  existing `Ctrl-D`/`Ctrl-U` half-page and `PageDown`/`PageUp`

Tree navigation gaps filled:
- `Home`/`End`/`G`/`PageUp`/`PageDown` now work correctly in Tree mode
  (previously fell through to main list navigation)

All 1,908 existing tests pass. Updated 3 snapshots for new header text.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
